selected_factors: Internal Cognitive Drift (mind wandering, low motivation)

overview.summary: Model rated concentration 1 point higher than user. Both identified location transitions as a key factor, but user also reported internal cognitive drift (unmeasured by sensors), which likely explains the lower self-assessment. Model may have overweighted positive audio/vitals signals while underweighting subjective cognitive state.

selected_factors: Internal Cognitive Drift (mind wandering, low motivation)

factor_sensor_mappings.factor: Internal Cognitive Drift (mind wandering, low motivation)

result.why_difference: Model correctly detected location transitions (586m displacement, 1995m travel) and favorable environmental conditions (43.69 dB, optimal heart rate), but assigned score of 7 based on observable sensor quality during stationary periods. User reported internal cognitive drift—an unmeasured latent state—which degraded subjective focus despite good external conditions. The 1-point gap reflects the model's inability to detect mind wandering or motivation issues that are not manifested in physiological or environmental signals.

prediction_agent_sensor_judgment.audio: Reliable and well-aligned. Low dB (43.69) and quiet/silence labels correctly indicated favorable acoustic environment. Continue using <45 dB threshold and label distribution as strong positive indicators. No recalibration needed.
prediction_agent_sensor_judgment.vitals: Heart rate (73.65 bpm) and minimal steps (6.3 avg) correctly indicated calm physiological state. However, these metrics cannot distinguish between engaged focus and passive mind wandering. Avoid treating stable vitals as definitive proof of high concentration; they indicate absence of physiological distraction only.
prediction_agent_sensor_judgment.motion: Stationary contexts (5 events, 0 active) correctly identified seated behavior. Aligned well with low-movement study pattern. Reliable for detecting physical restlessness but cannot infer cognitive engagement. Maintain current interpretation.
prediction_agent_sensor_judgment.gps: High displacement (586m) and travel (1995m) correctly flagged location transitions. Model appropriately penalized for transition time. However, the contradiction between GPS travel and stationary motion was well-resolved as discrete location changes. GPS interpretation was accurate; maintain current logic for distinguishing commute vs. restless movement.

judgment_policy_next_run: When audio, vitals, and motion all indicate favorable conditions but user reports lower score, increase suspicion of unmeasured cognitive drift rather than assuming sensor-based score is ground truth.
Cap concentration score at 6.5-7.0 range (not higher) when location transitions occur, even if stationary-period conditions are excellent, to account for cognitive disruption from context switching.
